1.description: Dates of policy changes, observational data, and field experiments. Acronyms: arranged overdraft (AOD), unarranged overdraft (UOD), unpaid item (UI), Competition and Markets Authority (CMA), Financial Conduct Authority (FCA), and randomized controlled trial (RCT).

2.description: Illustration of balance thresholds at which alerts are sent. Acronyms: arranged overdraft (AOD), unarranged overdraft (UOD), and unpaid item (UI). The AOD limit is preagreed between bank and consumer, whereas the UOD limit is unknown to the consumer. *If the consumer does not have an AOD facility, this will be a UOD/UI alert. **If the consumer does not have a UOD facility, this will be a UI alert.

3.description: Experiment timeline. Acronyms: arranged overdraft (AOD), unarranged overdraft (UOD), and unpaid item (UI). Month 1 is May 2017. Baseline alerts are introduced to control groups and treatment groups at the same time. Policy mandated automatic enrollment into just-in-time UOD and UI alerts prior to month 10 (February 2018). These alerts were added to the baseline during the experiments to comply with the deadline. Bank A used design A for all of its experiments (treatments 1, 7, 10, and 11). Bank B used design B1 for all its experiments except the tests of just-in-time UOD and UI alerts (treatments 2, 5, 6, 8, and 9). To comply with the regulatory deadline, these alerts were tested using a staggered-rollout approach as shown in design B2 (treatments 3 and 4). Treatment alerts are introduced to all treatment groups in month 7 and, in the staggered-rollout design B2, to control groups in month 10.

A1.description: Distribution of overdraft charges. These graphs show the distribution of customers' monthly arranged overdraft charges (Panel A) and unarranged overdraft and unpaid item charges (Panel B) for each of the United Kingdom's six major banks in 2015. Graphs are based on observed charges in pounds sterling per month. In each panel, the left-hand graph shows the average fraction of the sample that has none of the relevant charges in a given month. The right-hand graph shows a kernel density of the relevant charges for ranges that exclude zero ((0,£50] for AOD charges and (0,£100] for UOD charges). The kernel densities are scaled to be unconditional (rather than conditional) on positive charges.
